title: Living Asia Channel — live segment
text: Espacio Verde offers a spacious swimming pool set right beside the river. There is also a kiddie pool for the little ones. From the river depth guests can try their hand at fishing like the locals do. With the bamboo pole, you may have to be patient, but the feeling of landing a fish comes as a thrill. It is also a pleasure to take a boat on a leisurely road down the river. A few rivers flow in and around Roja City. It's no wonder that it's such an aquatic city. You'll get to feel that even more when you get to go on the Kadimaha River Tour. It involves a two- or three-hour excursion on a pleasure raft, or cabana. While embarking on the river tour, you can also take part in a few different pastimes. You may indulge in grilled fish, steamed crab, crisp anchovies and fresh coconut. All the while, you'll be serenaded with traditional folk music. On the raft is a team of wellness technicians ready to pamper your body via massage and manicure. Now wouldn't you love to escape for a few hours on a river cruise like this one? Here at Go Global Realty, we value the relationships with our customers. I'm Michael Ring, I'm the co-owner of Go Global Realty. Wherever you are in the world, you can give us a call at Go Global Realty and we'll be able to assist you in purchasing real estate in the United States. You can purchase it for investment property or for personal use. Hello, I'm Ronnie Chen, the owner of Go Global Realty. I hope that we'll be able to assist you in purchasing real estate. Hello, I'm Adriana, the owner of Go Global Realty. Hello, I'm Ronnie Chen, the owner of Go Global Realty. I'm the co-owner of Go Global Realty. Hello, I'm Adriana, the owner of Go Global Realty. Give us a call at Go Global Realty. We speak over 12 languages and look forward to helping you in your real estate needs. Hello, this is Keith Evans at the Lion Habitat Ranch. We'd like you to come out and visit our ranch. There's 38 lions, a giraffe that paints, hostages, e-moods, and birds. We do school tours, general emissions, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, and Monday. And behind the scenes tours every day of the week. Besides coming in as general emission, you can also pay extras to help our animals. And you can feed the giraffe. You can feed one of the lions. Or you can have Ozzie paint a custom canvas for you. You can also buy Ozzie painting to the gift shop. While we do our demonstrations at 12 and 2 of Ozzie painting, all those canvases are available for purchase. In addition to everything you can do here in person, you can find us on the website lion habitat ranch.org where you can make your reservations online or buy your paintings online. Thank you very much. Please come. Buying, keeping, or selling your home is one of the biggest decisions you and your family need to make in your lifetime. Specify a home inspector trusted by the Greater Las Vegas Association of Realtors. F-10 Inspection Services has been serving the Las Vegas real estate industry for many decades which secures all inspections with a $10,000 honor guarantee backed by the International Association of Home Inspectors. F-10 Inspection Services, proudly Asian American. Call 702-374-7377 or visit f-10.com now. It is fair to say that Rojas City has a robust tourism infrastructure but let's not forget it's also a center of trade and commerce. Rojas is a unique homegrown industry in Capiz Shell Handicraft. Here we see the workshop of Capiz Atibaba which makes decor for export. Countless pieces of luminous Capiz are being arranged into ornate creations. Many of the decorations they build can be lit from within. The glow enhances the alluring mother-of-pearl translucent. Far off on the easternmost part of the province, a similar home-spun business can be found in the town of Bilal. A private residence houses the ghee crafts Shell Museum and Gallery. Seashells in many forms and patterns are set on display. A small workshop has been put up where crafters work to assemble the shelves in constructing home decor. Many people in Capiz make their livelihood out of some home-spun business. Another local cottage industry involves the manufacture of steel cooking pots, the type often marketed to budget conscious families. You can see that it takes muscle and manpower to get the cooking pots into proper market-ready transition. Fishing is another common source of livelihood in Capiz. Along the shore in Ivesan township, you may come across fisherfolk who have brought in the day's catch, sorting it before taking the catch to market. They are likely to take the fish to Ivesan Bug Safan Center, Trading Point. And you'll find fresh fish and other marine produce in all the town markets and in the sprawling wet market in Wahas city. Alongside an amazing variety of fresh fish, you may find cubfuls of prawns, scallops, cockles, triangots, shell clams, live crabs, even sea grapes. The market is also stocked with sugary treats. Sugar is an important commodity here. The sugar industry is huge in Capiz. Let us visit a site in the municipality of President Rojas, where a sugar mill got built back in 1924. Now the site is occupied by a massive industrial mill run by Capiz Sugar Central. Tons of sugar cane are delivered here to be processed into the type of sweetener you can add to your coffee. The tourism industry in Capiz can still use a bit more development outside of Rojas city. You do find a few resorts, perhaps the best known being the Suhod Spring Resort in Dumalang township. The most striking fast of the resort is the presence of a large cavern, Suhod Cave. It is open to exploration. Out of the cave flows a stream of natural spring water with a touch of sulfur. The water is good for taking a dip in and it's said to be incredibly rejuvenating. You can rent the hut, picnic by the spring or else go on a nature walk. Other inland resorts in Capiz include Lollets Eco Park in the municipality of Dau. The Garden Lake Lollets Eco Park is actually situated by a natural lagoon. But Lollets does offer adult and child swimming pools more readily accessible to the casual holiday goer. Over in the municipality of Pontevedra, check out the Maribird inland resort. Maribird is distinguished by its enormous resort class pool known to be the biggest swimming pool in the entire province. If you didn't pack a picnic lunch, you may order fresh refreshments, have a chicken's cordon blue, steam crab or a zesty salad. Also located in Pontevedra is Villia Consorcia, a resort with a peaceful garden setting. It's a relaxing place for dining. Try the seafood paella, it's excellent. Villia Consorcia has an outdoor swimming pool with a corkscrew water slide over a meter and a half in height. If a beach getaway is what you're looking for, travel to the coastline along Ivisan Township. It is about an hour and a half from Roja City. Here you'll come across Bachao Beach, a strip of billowy white sand almost as fine as the sugar that come from copies. This place is sometimes called Virgin Beach, but it is hardly untraveled. At summertime, people flock here from the cities. Out in the sea, you will spot a number of rocky islands. You can hire a fishing boat and take the better part of the day hopping from one island to another. It can be a glorious way to spend a summer's day. Hello, this is Keith Evans at the Lion Habitat Ranch. We'd like you to come out and visit our ranch. There's 38 lions, a giraffe that paints, ostriches, e-moos, and birds. We do school tours, general admission, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, and Monday. And behind the scenes, tours are available on the website. Besides coming in as general admission, you can also pay extra to help our animals. You can feed the giraffe, you can feed one of the lions, or you can have Ozzie paint a custom canvas for you. You can also buy Ozzie painting to the gift shop. While we do our demonstrations at 12 and 2 of Ozzie painting, all those canvases are available for purchase. In addition to everything you can do here in person, you can find us on the website. LionHabitatRanch.org where you can make your reservations online or buy your paintings online. Thank you very much. Please come. Heritage Tourism and Copy stakes the form of visits to old churches. Saint Martin of Tours Parish Church is a fine-looking limestone edifice located in the municipality of Tumala. The Spanish-era church was constructed in 1883. In the township of the O is an older church, Saint Pithamas of Villanueva, which dates back to 1865. In the municipality of Panay is Santa Monica Parish Church, which has been declared a national historical landmark. The basic structure of the church dates to 1774, but it was severely damaged by storms in 1867. It was then restored in 1884. Take good note of the outer walls, composed of coral stone. Ask for permission before you climb the 69 steps up the bell free where you can view a collection of antique church bells. The centerpiece is known to be the biggest Catholic church bell in Asia. The townspeople call it Takum. Takum. Takum. Takum. The townspeople call it Takum餅gane, which means the big bell. Seventy sacks of coins provided the metal used in casting it. Beside the church is Nuseo di Santa Monica. In here, you'll see doors that came from the earliest church building. The vault holds an impressive collection ofartic... Many are centuries old. One majestic altar made of pure silver is 400 years old. Another fine altar is covered in gold. Vestiments, tabernacles, other religious counterments are on display. One item stands out, an antique accordion, and even works. Capis has some pilgrimage sites geared toward religious tourism. As a bonus, they can offer magnificent countryside views as they take you up high places. One pilgrimage point located in Pilar is called Agdalin Shrine. When you get there, you may want to buy flowers to give as an offering. Then you'll go trudging on the many, many steps going up Agdalin Hill. There you will be greeted by a towering figure of the Virgin Mary. Another pilgrimage point, our Lady of Lords Grotto at Meditation Hills, may be found in the municipality of Saptean. Inspiration to build a place came to the founder in a dream. To go on the pilgrimage, you need to climb a flight of ivory-covered steps leading through the thick roves of trees. The path will take you to the top of Repentance Hill, where a white cross pierces the sky. High up there, feel free to meditate upon the lovely panorama of forests and fields laid out in front of you. Here at Go Global Realty, we value the relationships with our customers. There's 38 lions, a giraffe that paints, hostages, emus, and birds. We do school tours, general emissions, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, Monday, and behind the scenes tours every day of the week. In addition to everything you can do here in person, you can find us on the website lionhabitat ranch.org where you can make your reservations online or buy your paintings online. Thank you very much. Please come. These days the big buy word for a copy's travel industry is ecotourism. The province is highlighting attractions that join recreation with environmental sustainability. One notable effort in Roja City is the Palina Greenbelt Ecotarp on Palina River. About 5 kilometers from the city proper, the ecotarp consists of bamboo huts and floating rafts, upon a diversely rich patch of the riverbank. The waters teem with the species of fish and of shellfish. Eight types of mangroves grow, populated by 34 varieties of birds. Visitors to Palina Ecotarp may go on a mangrove tour, a river cruise, even fishing or swimming. It's a carefree throwback to quaint old days of true countryside living. Another mangrove ecotarp also offers that sweet taste of country-style life. The joint project of Copy State University and the local community, the park acts as a nursery for mangrove plants and a hatchery for crabs. Careful footing is required to get across the bamboo pathway, leading through the marsh. A band of crab fishermen may be on hand to help you test your skill at catching a crab. When you have caught one, a fisherman quickly binds the crab so it won't run, then into the cook product zone. The fisherfolk also steam up some clams and other shellfish. A lunch spread is prepared displaying the rich bounty of the river marsh and neighboring fields. Fresh fish, sweet clams, prawns, banana, cassava cake and a lot more. Everyone's gathering around the table with Dine combined style, with your hands. It's the Filipino way of enjoying your meal. Be sure to find time to take in the beauty of the surroundings. The river looks very inviting. Why not push off on a raft and experience it up close? As you gently float, you may even feel a deep sense of contentment coming over you. This is what they mean when people talk about a countryside idol. If you're looking for a wilder countryside adventure, travel to the municipality of Hamindan inside Camp Peralta. The third largest military camp in the Philippines. You'll discover Kalikasan Nature Park, a new ecotourism zone taking up most of the camp's 33,300 hectares. The park's wood are largely untouched by development, other than a bit of infrastructure. A short trek down a steep flight of steps takes you to the scheduled hideaway of one of the park's wonders, Kalikasan Waterfall. It's one of the five falls that are concealed throughout the wilderness. Many of them were accidentally discovered on the course of military training in the forest. If you are feeling adventurous, push a little deeper into the wilderness on a more difficult trek along the river. When you see a clump of boulders jutting out of the water, you'll know you're close to your goal. A magnificent view of Maninamun Falls. A series of graceful cascades flowing over a cluster of rocky outcrops. It's a place in which to stand in awe of the majesty of nature. Maninamun Falls offers yet another glimpse at Kapi's water enchantments. Water, life-giving water, is intrinsic to the appeal of the province. River waters that buoy you on idyllic journeys. Spring waters that bring rejuvenation and fun. Resort waters that impart leisure and delight. Water once nourished the coral stones that formed the body of old churches. An old water tank now houses precious artifacts of history. The great Sibuyan Sea cradles the islands and washes against the beaches and the bounty of the sea enriches the dinner table. Seafood of unwavering quality has made Kapi's a hotspot for culinary experiences. Our memories of Kapi's shimmer like a pearl, as gorgeously captivating as Kapi's shells themselves. We hope you will be inspired to travel to the province of Kapi's in the Philippines and create captivating memories of your own. One of the six provinces of western Visayas, copies is surrounded by Sebuyan Sea in the north, Antique in the west, Aklan in the northwest and Iluido in the southeast. Deep like an open palm, the province of copies welcomes travelers to its 16 municipalities and its vibrant provincial capital, Rojas City. Within its two districts lies a unique interplay of culture, adventure, industry and cuisine, dominated by the bounty of the sea and its scenic coastlines. The provincial capital, Rojas City, was named after the country's fourth president, Manuel A. Rojas. The province,
